rationale.R1.1: Section advances a strong contribution claim ('first procedure') and an explicit gap statement about poor calibration; reviewing requires reconstructing and stress-testing both.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain — slow-converging nuisances → under-coverage → exchangeability-based calibration fix — must be internally consistent and free of unstated leaps.
rationale.R1.3: Precise quantitative claims are stated (within 1.5pp of 95%, under-coverage of 8–15pp, <30% width inflation on three benchmarks); verifying these against reported experiments is load-bearing — misalignment here invalidates the section's empirical case.
rationale.R1.4: 'First procedure' is a strong novelty claim; the related-work paragraph positions CalDML against five specific prior lines and must accurately characterize what those methods do and do not provide.
rationale.R1.7: Five named citations (Robins & Rotnitzky 1995, Chernozhukov et al. 2018, Wager & Athey 2018, Künzel et al. 2019, Kennedy 2023) plus Smith & Todd 2005 need accuracy checks; coverage of conformal-inference precursors relevant to the calibration step may also be incomplete.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize evaluating the scientific and technical soundness of the arguments and empirical claims (yes), or the quality and clarity of the writing and prose (no)?
